item.title: Jackson USA Concert Bass 1990 — $2,995
item.description:
$2,995 · Fair · Tigon Tactical LLC

1990 Jackson USA Concert Bass – Neck-Thru – Custom Sparkle – Rare Pre-Fender Era –

For sale is a very rare 1990 Jackson USA Concert Bass. This is a true pre-Fender Jackson from the golden era, made before the company was sold to Fender in 2002.

These were essentially Custom Shop level instruments built in very limited numbers at the famous San Dimas factory. They rarely come up for sale.

Key Features:

•  Neck-through-body construction

•  Factory custom black sparkle finish with real mother of pearl shark inlays

•  Real mother of pearl Jackson logo on headstock

•  Ebony fretboard

•  EMG pickups

•  Original Jackson hardshell case included

•  Brand new Ernie Ball strings just installed

This is a true collector piece from one of Jackson’s best periods. The custom sparkle finish shows some normal checking and cracking for a 36-year-old instrument, but it does not affect playability whatsoever.

Plays great, sounds killer, and looks extremely cool.

Local pickup in Miami preferred. Shipping available at buyer’s expense.

Serious buyers only — this one won’t last long.

Key Specifications & Features

  • Neck: Maple with a comfortable slim '60s profile

  • Production: Strictly limited to 1,000 units worldwide

item.title: Fender Steve Harris Artist Series Signature Precision Bass - Olympic White — $1,668.83
item.description:
$1,668.83 · Excellent · Jimmy's 80s Gear Depot

Description

Steve Harris's galloping fleet-fingered basslines have turbocharged U.K. metal titans Iron Maiden for decades and have made him the most influential metal bassist alive. Harris has stayed true to his battle-hardened Precision Bass over the years, and his signature Steve Harris Precision Bass now comes in his famously regal gloss White finish with special pinstriping, mirrored pickguard and West Ham United F.C. crest. Other ironclad features include a single powerful Seymour Duncan® Steve Harris signature model pickup, Fender HiMass™ bridge, Fender round-wound strings and Harris's signature on the back of the headstock. A set of Rotosound® Steve Harris Signature flat-wound strings are included in the gigbag.

Features

  • Gloss White finish with maroon-blue-maroon pinstriping and West Ham United F.C. crest
  • 7.25"-radius maple fingerboard with 20 jumbo frets and black plastic nut
  • Seymour Duncan® Steve Harris SPB-4 signature model pickup
  • Flat-top knurled chrome control knobs
  • Fender High-Mass bridge
  • '70s-style Schaller® tuning machines
  • Fender 7250M .045-.105 Nickel Plated Steel round wound strings

From　Official site

Steve Harris Precision Bass® – Fender

Excellent used condition.
Includes the original soft case and accessories.

item.title: Warmoth Jazz Bass Deluxe Custom — $1,995
item.description:
$1,995 · Excellent · Soulful Gear Spot

Wicked 70’s jazz bass sounds! A full chambered body made by Warmoth with a figured walnut top and a one-piece swanp ash body. Tortoise shell binding throughout the top body. Satin finished by Warmoth. This was a very expensive body alone.
Equipped with Bartolini B axis pickups and Nordstrand 3 band EQ preamp. Neck was made by Freestone guitars up state NY. Incredible neck! This bass is seriously one of a kind in many ways and playability is as good as my Sadowsky basses.

Will ship with a gig bag and well packaged to the US 48 lower states only. Feel free to ask any questions.

item.title: Spector Euro 4 LT 2018-2019 - Red Fade Gloss — $2,542.84
item.description:
$2,542.84 · Excellent · Matt's Bass gear

The EuroLT model is based on Spectors iconic NS-curved body and through-neck construction, featuring a figured maple top and a European alder back, with weight relief applied to the wing sections.
The three-piece maple neck features an ebony fingerboard and a precisely engineered tapered neck profile to increase rigidity, successfully combining comfortable playability with the powerful low-end sound characteristic of a through-neck bass.
The pickup features a specially custom-wound Bartolini pickup and a custom-tuned preamp.

Condition Grade - Excellent: Some very minor, hardly visible scratches as shown, but no issues for normal use. Im only selling this bass due to after having this bass for around 5 years, I've decided the style and design of the bass is not for me and purchasing something new. The bass is incredible and deserves to be played regularly.

Serial Number: NB15386
Country of Manufacturer: Czech Republic
Case: Genuine Spector soft case included
Weight: 3.98 kg
Truss Rod: No issues
Remaining Fret Life: Within normal range; fret height is sufficient for playing
Scale: 34"

Features:

  • 4-string production version of the legendary Spector NS bass
  • 3-piece maple neck-through design delivers snap, speed, and sustain
  • Darkglass active preamp system with bass and treble controls, internal mid-control trimpot, and bridge and middle volume controls
  • European alder body with weight relief that your shoulder will appreciate
  • Custom Bartolini pickups deliver high output with exceptional punch and definition

item.title: Rickenbacker 4001 1977 Mapleglo - LEFT HANDED — $3,416.98
item.description:
$3,416.98 · Very Good · Steven's Gear Garage

For sale is a beautiful 1977 left handed 4001 bass. It is a black plastic mapleglo, all original except for the bridge pickup which was replaced with a seymore Duncan. The original bridge pickup is included, however, needs to be rewound.


I am the third owner of this bass. It was originally purchased in the 70s by a fellow in the Uk, then made its way to a buyer in Edmonton before making its way to me.

A great piece of history, and a spectacular vintage bass with a nice thin neck.

There is some light buckle rash on the backside, and a few small dings here and there. It’s almost 50 years old!

item.title: Left-handed lefty 1988 Bass Collection SB501 by SGC Nanyo Japan bass + case, VERY RARE — $1,128.74
item.description:
$1,128.74 · Excellent · Arkad

A+ Excellent condition.

From a private collection (collector's item).

Comes with the hard case shown in the photos.

Bass Collection by SGC Nanyo bass guitars are well-regarded, Made in Japan, high-end instruments introduced in the late 1980s. They are best known for their Japanese build quality, lightweight contours, slim necks, modern design, versatile electronics, and superb timber selection and craftsmanship.

This is a high-range model SB501 by Bass Collection by SGC Nanyo.

Sold for £760 in the early 1990s (please see photos – attachments to the auction).

Features:

• Active 2-band low-impedance EQ (+/- 15dB treble/bass cut and boost)

• Heel-less bolt-on 3-piece Maple “sandwiched” neck

• 2-octave Ebony fingerboard

• Beautifully grained solid exotic Ash body, looks like 1-piece (Japanese Sen Ash) – please see photos

• SGC Nanyo P/J pickups

• Gotoh Japan machine heads

• Unique Gotoh Japan 6-way adjustable and lockable bridge

• Four knobs (Volume, Pickup Balance, Treble Boost/Cut, Bass Boost/Cut)

• Black hardware

• 34” scale

Superb playability, lightweight, and very comfortable.

More photos to come soon.

item.title: 1982 B.C. Rich Bich 8-string Bass Black Case OHSC Made in USA Real Deal Case Queen — $7,995
item.description:
$7,995 · Very Good · Only Heavy Only

This Bich model 8-string bass is a superb example of the might & majesty that was B.C. Rich in the late 70’s & early 80’s. It is in exceptional condition for its age, with just three very small dings in the finish that are through to the wood – one on the front lower horn, one on the back lower horn, and once on the back edge of the lower curve, which appears to have been finished over in clear. I would grade this instrument as near-mint, but please check the images & judge this for yourself.  

The finish is solid black, but is very thin & almost an “open-pore.” The grain of the wood underneath can be seen from certain viewing angles, although I was not able to capture this on camera due to the reflective properties of the finish.

From the grain patterns, it appears to me that the body wings are Koa with a Maple neck, which was a common combination for this model when they were being produced. The instrument weights 9.5 Lbs.

This is a 30.5” short-scale instrument.  It has a brass nut, Grover Imperial machine heads, Badass Ibridge, dual DiMarzio Model-P pickups, and the full Moser Bich electronics with dual preamps.  The electronics have been tested & are fully operational, but the Varitone and mini switches could stand to be cleaned.  

The Badass bridge has a fair amount of oxidation on the top that has eaten through the chrome – I find this strange considering how pristine the rest of the instrument is.However, it is fully functional and even the octave strings intonate perfectly – surprising considering that they share saddles with the root strings.

The neck is straight and the frets are in excellent condition. I adjusted the truss rod and the nut is turning in both directions with minimal force.  

Note that there are not anchor screws for the octave string machine heads. There are no drill holes present for these, so I assume that the factory did not deem them necessary.

The instrument plays well and sounds great.  It comes with the original hardshell case which is also in excellent condition with just some tears in the vinyl along the top & bottom edges.

All in all, I feel that this is a collector-grade instrument that is a rare an wonderful find in this day and age.

item.title: Parker Fly FB-5 Bass 2003 - Rare Ken Parker Era — $4,750
item.description:
$4,750 · Excellent · Egg Shen's Bus Tours

Near perfect condition. Minor pick scuffs and buckle rash that can easily be taken out.

This is one of the rare FB-5s that were built in the Willmington, MA factory during the Ken Parker era between 2002 and early 2003. They made less than 60 basses during the time Ken owned the company. This one was made in March of 2003. Any made after June of 2003 were made by USM and not in MA. This is considered a pre-refined Parker.

I bought this on a whim about 6 months (GC online) ago because I wanted to see one in person as I collect, repair, and resell Parkers only and have never had a chance to see one of these in person. It is beautiful! And is in near perfect condition. Once I realized it was a pre refined, I decided to hold onto it... But I only play guitar so this is too much for me and just sits there looking pretty.

I will try and make a video of this as I always due with my listings, but feel free to ask any questions.

The other ones listes currently are in very poor condition and are refined era models made by USM.

This is a collector quality bass.

Comes with original Soft case (see photo).

item.title: Schecter Stiletto-4 Stealth Pro EX bass in Satin Black — $1,599
item.description:
$1,599 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Stiletto Stealth-4 Pro EX Bass Satin Black

The Stiletto-4 Stealth Pro EX delivers a refined playing experience with its thin 'C' neck profile and 35" scale that balances comfort with extended reach. The neck-thru construction with Ultra Access body design provides seamless access to upper frets while maintaining the tonal coherence of a solid mahogany build. This is a modern workhorse built to handle session work, live performance, and studio demands.

Playability & Build

The mahogany neck with maple stringers and dual carbon fiber reinforcement rods resists warping and maintains stability under tension. At just .787" thick at the first fret, the neck feels immediate and responsive in hand, while the 24 X-Jumbo frets and 16" fretboard radius support both precise intonation and aggressive playing styles. The Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut at 1.496" width provides solid string spacing without sacrificing ergonomic comfort.

Electronics & Tone

The combination of EMG MMCS bridge pickup and EMG P neck pickup delivers modern clarity and punch across the frequency spectrum. The volume/blend control paired with a 2-band EMG EQ gives you surgical control over your tone, from tight, focused lows to articulate highs, with enough onboard EQ to shape your sound without relying on amp settings.

Key Features
  • Body Material: Mahogany
  • Neck Construction: Mahogany with Maple Stringers and 2 Carbon Fiber Reinforcement Rods
  • Neck-Thru with Ultra Access design
  • Neck Shape: Thin 'C' profile
  • Scale: 35"
  • Fretboard: Indian Ebony with 24 X-Jumbo frets
  • Fretboard Radius: 16" (406mm)
  • Bridge Pickup: EMG MMCS / Neck Pickup: EMG P
  • Controls: Volume/Blend/2-Band EMG EQ
  • Hardware: Black Schecter Custom Fully-Adjustable Bridge with TonePros "M Series" tuners
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Black Tusq (1.496" width)
  • Inlays: Offset Dots with Luminlay side markers

The Stiletto-4 Stealth Pro EX combines precision hardware, premium electronics, and a meticulously engineered neck to deliver consistency, tone, and playability at the professional level.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Custom-5 bass in Natural Satin — $949
item.description:
$949 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Stiletto Custom-5 Electric Bass Gloss Natural

The Stiletto Custom-5 delivers a slim, fast neck profile with its thin 'C' shape and 35" scale that balances extended range with comfortable playability across all five strings. The mahogany body topped with flamed maple and walnut presents a visually striking instrument that feels substantial without excess weight. Built in Indonesia with a 6 bolt-on neck joint, this bass combines modern hardware and active electronics for articulate, punchy tone.

Playability & Neck Design

The thin 'C' neck profile measures .787" at the first fret and .866" at the 12th, enabling quick position shifts and reduced hand fatigue during extended sessions. A 35" scale accommodates the low B string with proper tension while the 16" fretboard radius and 24 X-Jumbo frets support both smooth legato playing and precise articulation, all topped with a Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut measuring 1.771" wide.

Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG 40HZ pickups paired with a 2-band active EMG EQ (Master Volume/Blend configuration) deliver tight, focused low-end and responsive mids ideal for modern bass styles. The 9-volt clip-in battery compartment powers the active circuit, and the satin gold hardware complements the natural finish while the S-Tek Dexterity bridge ensures stable tuning and precise intonation.

Key Features
  • Body: Mahogany with Flamed Maple and Walnut top
  • Neck: Maple/Walnut multi-ply, thin 'C' profile, 6 bolt-on construction
  • 35" scale length (889mm) with 16" fretboard radius
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets on rosewood fretboard
  • Dual EMG 40HZ pickups with 2-band active EMG EQ
  • Master Volume/Blend and bass/treble controls
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 1.771" width
  • S-Tek Dexterity bridge with satin gold hardware
  • Schecter tuners and metal knurled knobs with set screws
  • 9-volt clip-in battery compartment
  • Includes Ernie Ball 5 String Super Long strings (.045–.130)
  • Right-handed configuration, Indonesia manufacture

This Stiletto Custom-5 combines a refined neck feel, punchy active electronics, and solid construction for 5-string players seeking reliability and modern tonal character.

item.title: Yamaha SB-2A Flying Samurai Bass MIJ 1966 Pearl Blue Medium Scale Vintage Japan w/ Hard Case — $2,095
item.description:
$2,095 · Very Good · A Flash Flood of Gear

This is a very rare and hard to find vintage made in Japan Yamaha SB-2A Flying Samurai Bass in pearl blue finish! This one was made in the mid to late 1960s, making this bass among some of the first Yamaha Flying Samurai's ever produced. This bass has a comfortable medium 32" scale length and the single neck pickup provides a very strong output, coming in at 9.8K ohms.  

This bass shows normal cosmetic wear for a vintage instrument that includes various surface scratches, scuffs, dents, and chips throughout the body with additional dings along the back of the neck and headstock. The hardware shows normal patina and the pearl blue finish has also slightly darkened from age. The reflective top of the volume pot has also fallen off and there is a previous hole from where the upper strap button used to be. The frets exhibit only normal play wear, and feel great up and down the neck. The truss rod still turns with plenty of room in both directions and the electronics work as they should. Overall, this bass is in really nice shape for its age!

This guitar comes freshly set up with D'addario EXL170M (45-100) Bass Roundwound strings. The bridge cover is missing and the strap button had been moved on the upper horn. This bass otherwise appears to be original. 

Includes the pictured hard case.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1972 - 1973 Custom Color Factory Black (4-Bolt, 100% Original Electronics) — $5,800
item.description:
$5,800 · Good · The Picker's Exchange

Up for sale is a highly collectible, remarkably honest 1972/1973 Fender Jazz Bass in a rare, factory-original Custom Color Black finish.

This bass sits in the highly desirable "sweet spot" window of early '70s CBS production: it features the classic 4-bolt neck plate configuration, bold black TV headstock logo, and a gorgeous bound rosewood fingerboard with white pearloid block inlays.

We have fully disassembled this instrument to provide complete transparency for collectors and players alike.

Electronics & Body Integrity:

  • The Potentiometers: The wiring harness is fully intact. The tone pot clearly shows the EIA source code 304-7218, verifying it as a Stackpole pot from the 18th week of 1972.
  • Solder Joints: The electronics feature untouched, dull factory solder joints with original wiring.
  • The Pickups: Features the original grey-bottom bridge pickup with correct factory ink stamp markings reading 5282.
  • Finish Authenticity: The factory black finish is confirmed genuine. Pulling back the pickguard shows raw wood in the screw holes and correct cavity routing with no sign of an undercoat or a different color underneath.

Factory Provenance (Fullerton Stamps):

This instrument features exceptional, well-documented builder pedigree stamps inside the wood joints:

  • Neck Heel: Cleanly stamped in green ink with the signature of legendary Fender craftsman K. KRAUSFELDT .
  • Neck Pocket: Features a highly legible, matching factory stamp from L. CRAWFORD alongside original red inventory matching dye.

Condition Disclosure & Transparency Statement:

We believe in full disclosure so you know exactly what you are buying. This bass is in Good/Very Good vintage condition with the following notable points:

  1. Pickguard: The underside of the white 3-ply pickguard has an aftermarket "Allparts" sticker on it . While it fits the body contours perfectly, please assume the pickguard is a high-quality vintage replacement.
  2. Play Wear: The body finish shows honest player wear, swirling, small nicks, and typical surface scratches consistent with a 50-year-old vintage instrument.
  3. Hardware & Accessories: The bass includes its original chrome bridge and pickup "ashtray" covers, original owner's manual (covering Telecaster Bass, Jazz Bass, & Bass V), vintage strap, and adjustment tools.
  4. Original Hard Case: Comes in its original vintage Fender black Tolex case with an orange plush interior. Please note that one of the outer flip-latches has broken away from the Tolex backing as pictured ), though the case still structurally protects the bass.

This is an absolute powerhouse of a 70s Jazz Bass that delivers the classic, crisp bridge pickup growl. It's verified from top to bottom, completely documented, and ready for its next home.

item.title: Ibanez Soundgear 6 string bass pearl white excellent cond — $300
item.description:
$300 · Excellent · anthony's Gear Depot

This listing is for one Ibanez Soundgear electric 6 string bass in pearl white or maybe metallic white, it's white with a subtle sparkle in the color. I've owned this bass for maybe 9 or 10 years now and played from it time to time and it's a great bass, but it could use some new strings. Curiously it's only marked as a Soundgear, while later models are marked with a specific model identifier such as sr506 or something like that. I believe this does have active electronics although I have never replaced the battery.

I play less and less bass now days and I have collected way too many instruments and this one has been sitting around collecting dust so it is time to let it go to someone who will play it regularly.

I've listed the shipping cost at $100.00, if it is less I'll send you the money back or only charge the actual shipping cost.

item.title: Jackson X Series RRB IV Rhoads Bass, Black with White Pinstripes U285169 — $799.99
item.description:
$799.99 · Very Good · Pixel Pro Audio

This used Jackson X Series RRB IV Rhoads 4-String Bass Guitar comes in a Black with White Pinstripes finish.

At A Glance
  • Case Included: No
  • Actual Instrument Weight: 8.1 pounds
  • Crafted In: Indonesia
  • Hand Dominance: Right-Handed
Condition

This used instrument is in very condition with some cosmetic wear, including a chip on the top of the headstock. This guitar has been fully inspected and it plays great. Please see pictures for details on item condition.

This guitar has been refurbished by the best refurbishing facility in the nation. Most of the instruments they refurbish come direct from the manufacturer with minor cosmetic imperfections, which are repaired as needed. If anything major was done to the guitar, it will be noted in the listing and shown in the pictures above. As part of their process, the guitar is issued a new serial number and has a small "used" stamp on the back of the head stock to prevent the guitar from being sold as new.

Product Details

When your low-end needs to cut through walls of distortion, the X Series RRB Rhoads delivers the sonic brutality that metal demands. This isn't just another bass – it's a weapon forged for the heaviest riffs and most punishing rhythms.

THE ICONIC RHOADS SILHOUETTE defines metal itself. That legendary pointed body shape screams aggression from every angle, draped in menacing gloss black with crisp white pinstripes. This bass commands respect before you even plug in.

The poplar body with neck-thru design delivers unmatched sustain and seamless playability. The maple neck features graphite reinforcement for rock-solid stability, while Jackson's signature pointy headstock makes an unmistakably metal statement that's been dominating stages for decades.

Experience lightning-fast playability with the 12"-16" compound radius amaranth fingerboard loaded with 21 jumbo frets and pearloid sharkfin inlays. The 34" scale delivers perfect string tension and devastating punch for crushing chords and blazing technical runs.

SONIC ANNIHILATION comes courtesy of the legendary EMG 35DC humbucker – the gold standard for metal bass aggression. This active powerhouse delivers earth-shaking low-end with crystal-clear definition that cuts through the heaviest mix. Simple volume and tone controls give you instant access to your perfect sound.

The Jackson hardtail bridge ensures rock-solid tuning stability and maximum sustain. No compromises, no weak links – just pure metal fury wrapped in sinister style.

The X Series RRB Rhoads: Where metal legends are born and stages are conquered.

  • Poplar body
  • Graphite reinforced maple neck-thru
  • Gloss neck finish
  • 12"-16" compound radius amaranth fingerboard
  • 21 jumbo frets
  • Pearloid sharkfin inlays
  • Jackson® sealed die-cast tuning machines
  • 34" scale length
  • Jackson® hardtail bridge
  • EMG® 35DC pickups
  • Volume and tone controls
Specifications

Neck

  • Headstock: Jackson® Pointed 4-In-Line
  • Headstock Binding: White
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Finish: Gloss Color Matched
  • Neck Construction: Neck-Through-Body with Graphite Reinforcement and Scarf Joint
  • Fingerboard Radius: 12" to 16" Compound Radius (304.8 mm to 406.4 mm)
  • Fingerboard Material: Amaranth
  • Position Inlays: Pearloid Sharkfin
  • Side Dots: Black
  • Number of Frets: 21
  • Truss Rod: Dual-Action Head Adjust
  • String Nut: Black Plastic
  • Nut Width: 1.625" (41.3 mm)
  • Nut Material: Black Plastic
  • Fret Size: Jumbo
  • Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)

Body

  • Body: Poplar
  • Body Finish: Gloss
  • Body Shape: Rhoads
  • Body Material: Poplar

Hardware

  • Bridge: Jackson® Hardtail
  • Control Knobs: Dome-Style
  • Hardware Finish: Black
  • Tuning Machines: Jackson® Sealed Die-Cast
  • Strings: Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.100 Gauges)
  • Strap Buttons: Standard

Electronics

  • Bridge Pickup: EMG® 35DC
  • Pickup Configuration: H
  • Controls: Volume, Tone
  • Switching: None

item.title: Schecter C-4 Standard 4-String Bass Guitar - Tobacco Burl — $379.99
item.description:
$379.99 · Excellent · Morris Guitar Company

The Schecter C-4 Standard boasts big, powerful bass tone with a look to match. The Tobacco finish and burl top gives it a sharp, modern style that stands out right away.

It has a mahogany body with a roasted maple neck and a 24-fret rosewood fingerboard, giving you a solid feel and plenty of room to move up the neck.

Two Schecter Diamond soapbar pickups deliver thick, full bass tone with plenty of output. Controls include two volume and two tone. If you're looking for strong low end and a modern look, the C-4 is hard to beat!

Why You’ll Love It

  • Schecter Diamond soapbar pickups for thick, powerful tone
  • Roasted maple neck with 24 frets
  • Mahogany body with burl top for a standout look
  • Tobacco Burl finish with black hardware

Please note: this instrument is stamped "used" on the back of the headstock and was given a new serial number by a manufacturer-authorized refurbisher. It may have at one time been deemed to have a cosmetic imperfection or to need a repair. It has now been expertly repaired, plays like new and shows little wear.

item.title: 1995 Yamaha MB-Special (MB-SPL) – Limited Edition – Rare "AMS" Amber Satin – MIJ – All Original — $999
item.description:
$999 · Excellent · Please take this stuff off my hands before I change my mind!

1995 Yamaha MB-Special (MB-SPL) – Limited Edition – Rare "AMS" Amber Satin – MIJ – All Original


The "Holy Grail" of the Motion B Series

Up for sale is a truly remarkable, collector-grade example of the Yamaha MB-Special, manufactured on September 9, 1995 (Serial: 4P09053). This is not your standard MB-III or MB-40; the "Special" was a limited-run production featuring a premium tonewood combination and high-mass hardware that elevates it far beyond the entry-level models.


A Connoisseur’s Provenance

This bass has a unique history. I didn't order this "willy-nilly" from an overseas exporter. I purchased this bass while living in Japan in 2013, personally selecting it for its superior feel and condition. Since then, it has been hand-carried and meticulously maintained in a temperature-controlled environment. It has never been subjected to the stresses of international freight or improper warehouse storage.

I am a long-time devotee of the Yamaha Motion B series. I started with an MB-40, then moved to an MB-65, but found it difficult to find one that wasn't "player-worn." I spent years searching for the definitive version of this bass, and my patience finally paid off with this MB-Special. While the later MB-75 is a fine instrument, I’ve always preferred the organic, growling response of a passive circuit over active electronics. This is all-analog, baby—just pure, high-output ceramic tone and incredible wood resonance.


Condition & Stability

At 31 years old, this instrument is in remarkable, "time-capsule" condition.

  • The Neck: Absolute stability. In over a decade of ownership, the neck has remained perfectly true and has never required a single truss rod adjustment.
  • The Hardware: All original gold-plated hardware. There is only ever-so-slight fading on a couple of small spots on the bridge plate—stunningly well-preserved for a mid-90s Yamaha.
  • The Finish: The Amber Satin (AMS) finish is the most desirable of the "Special" run, featuring a gorgeous figured maple top over a mahogany back. It has been kept clean and matte, preserved by regular cleaning and hydration of the rosewood board.
  • One small ding on the back, near the bottom strap holder. See photo.


Technical Specifications

  • Body: Figured Maple Top / Mahogany Back (The "Special" tonewood upgrade)
  • Neck: Maple (Scarf Joint / Bolt-on) – Rock-solid construction.
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood, 22 Frets (250R)
  • Scale Length: 820mm (Medium Scale) – Famous for its fast, comfortable playability.
  • Pickups: Original YBS-F4PC & YBS-F4JC (Ceramic) in the classic PJ configuration.
  • Bridge: Original Yamaha OGEB-205 Gold Locking Bridge. A heavy brass unit that provides incredible sustain and tuning stability.
  • Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone, and Pickup Balancer.


Includes: Premium "Cool Z" Gig Bag

The bass comes housed in a premium-quality "Cool Z" Japanese brand gig bag. Cool Z is the high-end house brand for Shimamura Music (manufactured by the legendary Fujigen factory). This bag offers professional-grade protection with reinforced padding, fitting for an instrument of this caliber.

Why Buy This Bass?

If you are looking for a "clean" investment or a world-class medium-scale player, this is the one. You are buying from an owner who knows the MB series inside and out. It is all-original, never messed with, and has been treated as a prized heirloom.


Let me know if you have any questions. Thank you.

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass 2026 - Diamond Dust Sparkle — $1,099.99
item.description:
$1,099.99 · Brand New · Musician's Warehouse Athens

Summary

75th Anniversary Precision Bass Collection

Introducing the 75th Anniversary Precision Bass® Collection: Celebrating three-quarters of a century of foundational tone and revolutionary design. Each 75th Anniversary Precision Bass model showcases the rich heritage and legendary tones that have made the P Bass® an unrivaled icon. From vintage purists to progressive players seeking cutting-edge features, rest assured there's a model that will deliver. Immerse yourself in the legacy of the Precision Bass and own a piece of Fender® history with the 75th Anniversary Precision Bass Collection.

Player II Sparkle Precision Bass

Experience modern craftsmanship with a touch of sparkle in the Player II Precision Bass. Crafted with a resonant alder body finished in eye-catching Diamond Dust Sparkle, this instrument delivers both style and substance. The Modern "C" shaped maple neck with 9.5" radius rosewood fingerboard, rolled edges, and medium jumbo frets provide the smooth, comfortable playability that today's players crave. The 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt™ Precision Bass® pickup delivers rich, powerful tones with enhanced clarity and punch that cuts through any mix. Other quality appointments include a 4-Saddle Bridge with Single-Groove Steel “Barrel” Saddles, open-gear tuning machines and a 4-ply pearloid pickguard with chrome hardware throughout. This exceptional 75th Anniversary Player II Sparkle Precision Bass combines contemporary appointments with a premium sparkle finish, perfect for players who want to stand out while delivering that legendary P-Bass sound.

Features

  • Contoured Alder Body
  • 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt™ Precision Bass® pickup
  • Modern "C" Neck Profile
  • 9.5“-Radius Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
  • Adjustable 4-Saddle Bridge With Single-Groove Steel "Barrel" Saddles
  • Commemorative 75th Anniversary Neck Plate

POSTAGE & SHIPPING NOTE:

- Upon cleared payment, all small items (pedals, wiring etc) are usually posted within 24-72hr


- Guitars and Basses are packed on Monday (courier collection Tuesday) and Thursday (for courier collection Friday)


Meaning, if your purchase is on a Thursday (after 1pm), Friday, Saturday, Sunday, or Monday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Tuesday.

If your purchase is on Monday (after 1pm), Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Friday.


We take great care and pride in making sure guitars are carefully set up and packed correctly for transport, so we do not rush this step.




The String Switch Noise store operates primarily as an online business.

However, we do have a dedicated showroom 30min south of the Brisbane CBD.

By appointment, we are available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm (and Saturday and Sunday by availability) to test out and pick up purchased items.

Please call or contact us directly through the website.

String Switch Noise



---Shipping---

--- International delivery is possible for most items and can be arranged at exact cost of shipping. However, there are some restrictions with regions and package size. Please contact us first BEFORE making any purchase to check if an item can be delivered, and to organise the ACTUAL delivery costs. International shipping pricing (if found on listing) is an estimate only (it may be more, but it also may be less). Any applicable customs, duties, or taxes that your country may charge is solely the responsibility of you the buyer. Any questions you may have about this should be directed to your local countries authorities.

--- We proudly strive to send out all orders within 24 hours of purchase and/or receiving cleared payment (apart from holidays and weekends). However please allow up to 72hrs for packing and courier pickups. Once your item is packed you will receive notification and relevant tracking number and courier/postal details.

--- Small items such as effect pedals and wiring harnesses are sent via Australia Post standard service, with tracking ONLY. Both domestically and internationally. No responsibility will be taken from us for the loss or damage of an item by Australia Post. At your request, signature upon delivery or postal insurance can be added for the additional cost. Please contact us before purchase, or immediately after purchase.

--- Australia wide, guitars are sent via courier delivery with full insurance, tracking, and signature upon delivery services. Guitar packages MAY be sent via Australia Post if the delivery area is in a remote region/outside normal courier delivery areas/outside normal costs (and the package size is under 105cm long).

--- Courier deliveries can NOT be sent to a PO Box, Parcel Locker, or Parcel Collect service. They must be sent to a physical address, where someone can sign for the item on arrival.

--- All items are packed to the highest degree for transit. Proudly, our packaging is at the highest of standards, with the best reputation you will find anywhere in the industry. This comes from 3500+ guitars (and counting) being sent without issue both Australia wide and internationally over 10+ years (and counting). A great amount of pride and detail goes into making sure every item arrives safe and in a timely manner. Pedals and small items are packed in multiple layering of bubble wrap and double corrugated shipping cardboard. Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

item.title: 1995 Fender FSR MIJ PB-57 '57 Precision Bass Reissue - Jupiter Blue - Pro Set Up w Perfect Frets/Action/Intonation! — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Very Good · The Guitar Dude

Fender FSR MIJ PB-57 '57 Precision Bass Reissue

Serial: S000719

Luthier Measurements: 

Nut Width: 1.68

Neck Thickness at 1st Fret: .84

Neck Thickness at 12th Fret: .98

Radius: 7.25

Scale: 34

Weight: 7 lbs 15 oz's!!

Comes in a Fender Gig Bag

$$$ It has just been professionally set up and dialed in for optimal tone and playability. It has low action with no buzzing. The frets are in perfect condition and polished to shine like a mirror, it is truly stage or studio ready/worthy. Including New Strings,  Perfect Intonation, Truss Rod Adjustment and Low Action Read Our Feedback and Rest Assured, The Dude Abides!


🌊 Circa 1995 Fender Japan PB-57 Precision Bass in Factory Special Run Jupiter Blue – Custom Mirror Pickguard Beauty! 🌊

Turn heads and drop jaws with this eye-catching Fender Japan PB-57 from the mid-90s in a rare factory special run Jupiter Blue finish. This ‘57-style Precision Bass reissue combines classic P-Bass thunder with bold Japanese craftsmanship and a one-of-a-kind custom upgrade that makes it truly unique.

Classic PB-57 Power Meets Standout Style:

•  Alder or equivalent resonant body in stunning Jupiter Blue – a vibrant, deep celestial blue that catches the light beautifully and stands out on any stage.

•  One-piece maple neck and fingerboard – delivering punchy attack, bright snap, and that signature articulate P-Bass growl with excellent note definition.

•  Classic split-coil Precision pickup for the timeless P-tone: tight, focused lows, midrange punch, and clarity that cuts through the mix whether you’re laying down grooves or slapping.

•  Vintage-style hardware, including the bridge and tuners, for authentic feel and rock-solid tuning stability.

The Custom Touch:

It features a striking custom mirror pickguard that adds flash and personality. The reflective surface complements the Jupiter Blue perfectly, creating a bold, stage-ready look that’s equal parts vintage and modern. It has some discoloration or staining from what looks like smokey clubs or bars.

Road-Tested with Authentic Character:

This bass has been played and loved. It shows plenty of scratches, dings, and wear – especially on the back – giving it genuine battle-worn charm and tons of mojo. These marks tell the story of gigs, rehearsals, and dedicated playing. The finish still pops, the neck feels great, and the overall vibe is that of a well-traveled instrument ready for its next chapter.

Despite the honest wear, this PB-57 has been professionally set up and dialed in for optimal tone, low comfortable action, perfect intonation, and effortless playability. It’s resonant, responsive, and ready to perform at a moment’s notice.

From classic rock and funk to indie, punk, or studio work, this Jupiter Blue PB-57 delivers the punchy, versatile Precision tone players crave while looking absolutely killer. Lightweight, balanced, and inspiring – it’s a bass you’ll reach for again and again.

Rare color + custom guard + Japanese quality = a player’s dream bass.

Serious bassists only – this standout won’t last long.

DM for more photos, videos, or to make it yours. Let this Jupiter Blue beauty keep grooving! 🎸🔵​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​



🔥 This guitar (or bass) has been professionally set up and dialed in for optimal tone and playability. Unless otherwise noted in the listing, It features low action with no buzzing or dead spots, perfect intonation, a brand-new set of American-made Ernie Ball strings, and a straight neck with a fully functional truss rod. Wr make sure all frets are leveled, dressed, crowned, and polished to a mirror shine. The fingerboard has been thoroughly cleaned (unfinished rosewood and ebony boards are treated with Fret Doctor, a premium all-natural oil). All electronics function flawlessly. The guitar has been deep-cleaned and detailed and arrives stage- or studio-ready. 🔥


About The Guitar Dude 

Three-time NAMM Top 100 Dealer worldwide. Specializing in premium Gibson, classic Fender guitars from all eras and the best Japanese made treasures. Known for the highest-quality consignment sales and custom layaway plans (available on select guitars and extendable beyond 45 days to fit your budget—contact us for details).  

Often copied, never duplicated. Follow us on Facebook & Instagram for beautiful guitar content. 


Consignment Services

Like what you see? Let us go to work for you. We offer the best and most fair priced consignment service on the planet! Look for me online. @TheGuitarDude


Our Guarantee  

We stand behind every guitar we sell. “Good enough” is never good enough. Every instrument is inspected, set up, and tested by multiple people immediately before shipping. We deliver the highest standard of quality and have built a reputation as a three-time NAMM Top 100 Dealer since 2009. The Dude Abides.  


Neck & Setup - (unless otherwise noted)

- Neck is perfectly straight with no truss-rod issues. 

- Intonation is spot-on.  

- Professionally set up with low action and new American-made strings.  

- All frets are dressed, leveled, crowned (if needed), and all frets on all guitars are polished to shine like new with no divots.  


Return Policy, Restocking Fees & No Tire-Kicking Policy 

We follow Reverb’s 7-day return policy. However:  

- You must 

notify us in writing within 3 days of delivery (per tracking) of your intent to return.  

- Original shipping and return shipping costs are not refunded unless the guitar is damaged in transit or is significantly not as described.  

- A 15% restocking fee applies to all returns for any reason other than damage in transit or significantly not as described.  


We do our best to honestly describe and photograph every guitar and most of the noticeable imperfections. That said, we are human, if something very small can be overseen, if tiny marks, dings or super fine scratches (normal wear) bother you, please don't buy.


We record demo videos of every guitar before shipping to document exact condition and performance. Additional photos, measurements, or in-hand descriptions are available upon request prior to purchase.  


Minor adjustments and tweaks to a set up (for example to correct a slight buzz or truss-rod tweak) are normal after shipping due to carrier mishandling the package, climate & atmospheric changes, especially when moving from South Florida to colder or less humid environments. These slight issues do not constitute “significantly not as described.” Guitars must be returned in the exact same condition as received/advertised or additional repair/restocking fees will apply.  


We are a small family owned and run business, not a big-chain retailer. We invest significant time, money and effort into every guitar. This is a NO TIRE-KICKING shop. There is no post purchase haggling, returns without restocking fees or partial refunds. If minor cosmetic marks, tiny imperfections, or the possibility of a post-shipping setup tweak makes you uncomfortable, please do not buy. Plenty of large retailers offer free trials and can absorb shipping costs—we do not.  


Payment, Shipping & International Duties  

- All items can be shipped internationally. Buyer is fully responsible for all import duties, taxes, and customs fees.  

- We accept PayPal, PayPal Credit, Affirm, and other major payment methods.  

- Most U.S. shipments are sent via UPS or FedEx with full insurance, tracking, and signature confirmation. All packages are professionally packed for maximum protection.  


International Shipping Note 

International shipping prices shown are for most countries. Certain countries have higher costs. We try and avoid USPS and mostly ship UPS, but USPS is the only available or reasonable carrier in certain situations. Express services (UPS, DHL, FedEx) are always available. We can’t under value merchandise as it can not be properly insured. If you would like to discuss different avenues, contact us by phone for a personalized quote.  


Thank you for considering The Guitar Dude. We look forward to earning your business and delivering a guitar that exceeds your expectations. Contact us anytime for questions, consignment inquiries, layaway details, or a personalized shipping quote.

item.title: Dingwall SP1-5 - Gloss Vintage Burst — $3,289.65
item.description:
$3,289.65 · Brand New · Doctorbass

The SP1 is the latest addition to our ever-expanding Ready To Play range and, as the model name suggests, draws much of its inspiration from the Custom Shop Super P. The initial brief was to come up with a more affordable instrument that combined elements of Leo Fender’s iconic blueprint with some of the innovative features for which Dingwall Guitars are now famous, including the customary multi-scale fingerboard.

Passive electronics with 4 way selector (Neck, Bridge, Both in Pararallel, Both in Series)

32" to 35" scale for a more traditional feel but still the advantages of multi scale design

22 fret neck for a more traditional feel and position.

 

BODY & NECK 

The first thing we did was to make the body profile thinner, so you get a lightweight body, but with a more solid tone. The body is alder, while the bolt-on neck is a single piece of maple. The rosewood fingerboard is flawlessly finished, with smoothly rounded edges and 22 banjo-sized frets. 

Aside from the multi-scale design, visually, the first thing you’ll notice that’s different about the SP1 is the scratchplate, which started out as a hand-painted design by Hugh Fisher. We’ve now digitized Hugh’s designs and had them UV printed. Each one is slightly transparent, so the colour of the body also influences the look of the pickguard. And because the pickguard material is transparent, we can print any colour.

“I don’t think it’s coincidental that the bassists who first recognized Dingwall’s potential were session guys who play and record bass day in, day out. These players need a versatile instrument that can deliver a studio-quality sound, be comfortable to play and remain straightforward to use. The Dingwall SP1 ticks all those boxes, and you can expect to see a lot more of them around in the future.” – Sheldon Dingwall

PICKUPS & ELECTRONICS 

The SP1 is passive, so there are no batteries or EQ boost, but the quality of the tonewoods, alnico-loaded pickups, and the overall construction mean that there’s absolutely no need for them. The front control is volume, and the back is a master tone. Between these is our Quad-Tone Pickup Selector. In its first position, fully clockwise, this selects the front pickup alone; one click clockwise is the front plus the back pickup in series, then in parallel; and finally, the back pickup on its own.

We spent years refining the pickup design for the SP1, and the end result has been so successful that we’re now using the same concept in our Custom Shop Super P.” – Sheldon Dingwall.“

HARDWARE 

The Dingwall Minimalist bridge features a custom riser system that’s designed to provide an increased range of flexibility when it comes to adjusting the action. If you play with a heavy technique, instead of raising your individual bridge saddles, which can put stress on the string and cause premature breakage, you can raise the entire bridge unit. That way you can move between a high action or a low action just by adjusting the shims, which leaves the saddles only for fine tuning. The tuners are lightweight, open-gear designs, and there is also a bullet string retainer to help maintain a decent break angle across the Graphtech nut.

DINGWALL STRINGS 

Dingwall strings have been meticulously tested to maximize performance and deliver a tone that blends effortlessly with any style of music. We were the first company to design strings specifically for multi-scale instruments. Available in both nickel and stainless steel, our strings provide an even tension for optimal balance, letting you experience the warmest highs and the tightest lows possible.

Note: basses are shipped with Dingwall Nickel Strings as standard.

item.title: Ibanez SR 1605 — $999.95
item.description:
$999.95 · Very Good · Parkway Music

The Ibanez SR1605 is a five-string Soundgear series bass featuring a poplar burl top mahogany body with a titanium-reinforced wenge and bubinga neck. It's equipped with Nordstrand Big Single 5 pickups, an Ibanez three-band EQ preamp, and premium hardware including Gotoh tuning machines and a GraphTech TUSQ nut. The versatile control layout includes volume, balancer, and a 3-way mid-frequency selector for precise tone shaping.

  • Original Gigbag Included

The pickup measures 11.18k, keeping the core vintage Precision Bass voice intact.Presented in overall fair condition, this bass has a non-original thumb rest, some non-original pickguard screws, extra thumb-rest screw holes in the body, and some reheated solder joints.
